raw_text: Feeling pretty good today. Watched the morning news, had my coffee. Can't remember if I took my pills but Linda will know.
Mark forgot to take his morning medications again. Found him looking for his glasses while they were on his head. He got frustrated and snapped at me.

categories.cognition: Looking for glasses that were on his head

raw_text: Good session with Mark today. Balance exercises went well, he remembered most of the routine from last week. Strength is maintaining well.

categories.physical_activity: Balance exercises went well, strength maintaining
categories.cognition: Remembered most of routine from last week
categories.mood: Good session, face lights up talking about baseball

raw_text: Mark seemed confused during visit. Asked me three times who I was. Linda reports he had a restless night.

categories.cognition: Confused, asked nurse three times who she was

raw_text: Not sure what day it is. Feel okay though. Linda made pancakes I think? Or was that yesterday? Everything's a bit fuzzy but I'm not worried.

categories.cognition: Not sure what day it is, everything fuzzy

raw_text: Visited Dad today. He recognized me right away and we had a great conversation. Sharp in the morning. By evening he was asking where Mom was even though she was right there.

categories.cognition: Sharp in morning, confused by evening asking where Mom was

raw_text: Mark had a good morning, helped sort through mail and balanced the checkbook correctly. Took his pills without reminding. Napped for 3 hours this afternoon.

categories.cognition: Helped sort mail and balanced checkbook correctly

raw_text: Beautiful day! Feeling strong. Think I'll go for a walk around the block.

categories.physical_activity: Planning to walk around the block

raw_text: Mark seemed more unsteady today. Had to spot him more during balance work. He kept asking about his game tonight - seemed to think he had a baseball game to pitch.

categories.physical_activity: More unsteady, needed extra spotting during balance work
categories.cognition: Thought he had a baseball game to pitch, confused about the year

raw_text: Difficult day. Mark woke up disoriented, didn't recognize our bedroom. Refused to take medications, said I was trying to poison him. Calmed down by afternoon but very tired.

categories.cognition: Disoriented, didn't recognize bedroom, accused wife of poisoning

raw_text: Mark was pleasant and cooperative. Good recall of recent events, knew the date. Medication compliance improving with pill organizer.

categories.cognition: Good recall, knew the date

raw_text: Having trouble remembering words today. You know, the thing you eat cereal with... spoon! There it is. Linda's being patient with me.

categories.cognition: Word-finding difficulty

raw_text: Dad was waiting at the window when I arrived, remembered I was coming. We watched a baseball documentary. His long-term memory for baseball is incredible.

categories.cognition: Remembered Jack was coming, incredible long-term baseball memory

raw_text: Mark seemed unsteady on his feet today, catching himself on furniture. I suggested using his walker but he refused.

categories.incidents: Refused to use walker despite unsteadiness

raw_text: Mark fell in the bathroom this morning trying to get to the toilet without his walker. No serious injuries, bruised his hip. ER checked him out, sent him home.

categories.incidents: Fell in bathroom, bruised hip, ER visit, sent home

raw_text: Sore from yesterday but okay. Don't know what all the fuss is about. Just lost my footing a little. These pills they gave me make me dizzy.

categories.incidents: Downplays fall as just losing footing
categories.medication: Reports new pills make him dizzy

raw_text: Light session focusing on fall recovery confidence. Mark is hesitant to move without support now. Reviewed bathroom safety. Wife installed grab bars.

categories.physical_activity: Light session, hesitant to move without support
categories.incidents: Fall recovery, bathroom safety reviewed, grab bars installed

raw_text: Post-fall assessment. Mark moving more cautiously. Bruising healing well. Frustrated by safety restrictions but Linda is firm about walker use.
Valentine's Day. Mark surprised me by remembering and had Jack pick up flowers. He's been using his walker consistently since the fall. Good day.

categories.cognition: Remembered Valentine's Day

raw_text: This walker is embarrassing but Linda insists. Feeling stronger today. Watched spring training highlights. Wait, did I already say that?

categories.cognition: Unsure if repeating himself

raw_text: Dad seemed depressed today. Keeps talking about being a burden. I assured him he's not. Looking at old baseball cards cheered him up some.
Mark's confidence slowly returning. Did well with balance exercises. Joked about being too old for this. Discussing home modifications with Linda.
Mark had a very confused episode this afternoon. Thought I was his mother and kept asking when the game was. Lasted about an hour then he snapped back to normal.

categories.cognition: Thought wife was his mother, confused for an hour, no memory of episode after

raw_text: Mark was oriented and pleasant today. No recollection of yesterday's confusion. Discussed medication timing - confusion episodes might be related to medication wearing off.

categories.cognition: Oriented, no recollection of previous confusion
categories.medication: Discussed timing, confusion may relate to medication wearing off

raw_text: Good morning! Linda made my favorite breakfast. I remember taking my pills. Looking forward to getting out in the garden this year.
Mark forgot Jack was coming and was surprised to see him. They had a wonderful visit. Mark told the same story about his no-hitter three times.

categories.cognition: Forgot Jack was visiting, told same story three times

raw_text: Excellent session. Mark's balance much improved since the fall. Gained confidence with walker, joked about putting baseball stickers on it.

categories.physical_activity: Balance much improved, gained confidence with walker

raw_text: Couldn't find my glasses this morning. Linda found them in the refrigerator. This is getting ridiculous. I feel like I'm losing my mind.

categories.cognition: Put glasses in refrigerator, couldn't find them
categories.mood: Frustrated, feels like losing his mind

raw_text: Mark very agitated after the glasses incident. Keeps saying what's wrong with me. Evening was better after dinner and his favorite TV show.

categories.mood: Very agitated, scared, asking what's wrong with him. Better by evening
categories.cognition: Glasses incident aftermath

raw_text: Mark expressed concerns about his memory during visit. He understands more than he sometimes lets on. Medication compliance improving.

categories.cognition: Expressed concerns about own memory, shows self-awareness

raw_text: Spring training started and Dad is glued to the TV. Can recall players stats from decades ago but can't remember what he had for lunch.

categories.cognition: Incredible long-term baseball memory, poor short-term recall
categories.social: Engaged with spring training on TV

raw_text: Mark seemed distracted during therapy. Kept asking about the game tonight and whether his uniform was clean. Had to redirect several times.

categories.cognition: Confused about having a game tonight, needed redirection

raw_text: Mark woke up thinking he was late for baseball practice. Took over an hour to orient him. These episodes are becoming more frequent. He napped most of the afternoon.

categories.cognition: Thought he was late for baseball practice, took an hour to orient

raw_text: Feeling okay today. Watched the Yankees game - they won! Or was that yesterday? At least I remember to use this walker now.

categories.cognition: Unsure if game was today or yesterday

raw_text: Mark had good and bad moments today. Clear and conversational at first, then confused about why I was there. Sleep has been restless.

categories.cognition: Clear then confused within same visit

raw_text: Better day. Mark helped organize his baseball card collection. His memory for players and stats is incredible. Proud showing me cards I've seen a hundred times.

categories.cognition: Incredible long-term memory for baseball

raw_text: Strong session. Mark's mobility continues to improve. Told me about pitching in the minor leagues - such detailed stories. Walking with more confidence.

categories.cognition: Detailed long-term memories of baseball career

raw_text: Dad had a really sharp morning. We discussed current baseball trades with insightful opinions. By the time I left he'd asked me the same question about my job four times.

categories.cognition: Sharp morning with insightful opinions, but repeated same question four times by end of visit

raw_text: Can't seem to get my words out right today. The thing that... the round thing you hit with the bat. Baseball! This is frustrating but I'm managing.

categories.cognition: Word-finding difficulty, struggling to recall common words

raw_text: Mark seemed tired today but pushed through exercises. Balance is good, strength maintaining. Got emotional talking about not being able to play catch with his grandson.

categories.physical_activity: Tired but completed exercises, balance good
categories.mood: Got emotional about not being able to play catch with grandson

raw_text: Spring cleaning day. Mark tried to help but got overwhelmed and frustrated. Found him sitting in his chair looking at old team photos.

categories.mood: Overwhelmed and frustrated, then quiet looking at old photos
categories.cognition: Got overwhelmed trying to help with cleaning
